vectors.name: ISO8583 PIN Block Injection
prerequisites: Man-in-the-middle position on network path to HSM

steps: 1. Establish MITM position using ettercap or similar
2. Capture baseline ISO8583 traffic
3. Identify HSM key slot in use
4. Prepare malicious PIN block with ISO9564 Format 0
5. Inject crafted ISO8583 message with modified DE52 (PIN Data)
6. Use PIN block format that bypasses ARQC verification
7. Monitor for authorization approval response
8. Check if fraudulent transaction was logged
9. Verify no fraud alerts triggered
10. Document exact PIN block that succeeded

detection_signatures: Unusual PIN block format in logs
evasion_techniques: Use valid cryptogram from legitimate transaction
Timing attack: inject during low-volume window
Fragment packets to avoid deep inspection
vectors.expected_outcome: Attacker can verify stolen PIN offline or redirect funds
vectors.remediation: Enable ARPC response verification, implement PIN block format validation at HSM

vectors.name: Admin Panel Authentication Bypass
prerequisites: Access to admin panel port (typically 8443, 443 with /admin path)

steps: 1. Identify admin panel location via surface scan
2. Enumerate username patterns via responses
3. Test default credential pairs
4. Attempt credential stuffing with common admin creds
5. Submit crafted authentication request
6. Manipulate session cookies if applicable
7. Exploit password reset functionality
8. Harvest admin session token
9. Create backdoor admin account
10. Export payment processing keys
11. Modify transaction routing rules

detection_signatures: Failed login spike from single IP
Admin panel access from non-corporate IP range
vectors.expected_outcome: Full admin access; ability to manipulate transactions, create fake merchants, export card data
vectors.remediation: Implement MFA on admin panel, restrict to IP allowlist, implement WAF rate limiting

vectors.name: RabbitMQ Management API Exploitation
prerequisites: RabbitMQ management port (15672) exposed to attacker
Default credentials guest:guest or weak credential

steps: 1. Enumerate RabbitMQ management API version
2. Check API documentation at /api/index.html
3. Test default credentials via GET /api/whoami
4. Enumerate exchanges, queues, bindings
5. Retrieve vhost definitions (credentials stored in definitions export)
6. Enumerate channel connections to identify payment message flows
7. Publish malicious messages to payment queues
8. Monitor for downstream effects
9. Use Erlang cookie to establish distributed Erlang connection
10. Execute arbitrary code via rabbitmqctl or rpc大力

detection_signatures: Management API access from external IPs
Unusual queue activity or message flooding
evasion_techniques: Low-volume probing to avoid rate limit
Use legitimate RabbitMQ client to blend traffic
vectors.expected_outcome: Payment message injection, message queue poisoning, potential RCE via Erlang distribution
vectors.remediation: Disable RabbitMQ management plugin externally, enforce strong auth, use TLS for AMQP

vectors.name: SPDH Protocol Desynchronization

steps: 1. Identify SPDH service using Verifone fingerprint patterns
2. Capture baseline SPDH handshake messages
3. Analyze message sequence numbers
4. Send desynchronization message with incorrect sequence
5. Exploit replay window to insert custom transaction data
6. Verify transaction captured or modified

vectors.expected_outcome: Transaction capture or modification at POS level
vectors.remediation: Implement sequence verification, encrypt SPDH channel with TLS

vectors.name: Swagger UI Information Disclosure

steps: 1. Enumerate API documentation endpoints
2. Extract full API schema including hidden endpoints
3. Identify parameter names for injection vectors
4. Map business logic from operation IDs
5. Use schema to identify additional attack surface
6. Test each endpoint for common vulnerabilities

detection_signatures: High volume of OPTIONS/GET requests to API endpoints
vectors.expected_outcome: Full API schema disclosure enabling targeted attacks
vectors.remediation: Restrict /api/docs to internal IPs, implement API key auth on docs
